nodes.title: Normalize input into a Date object

anchor.snippet: date instanceof Date ? date : new Date(date)

nodes.why: This supports both Date instances and timestamps in one code path.
nodes.tradeoff: More flexible API, but still relies on runtime date parsing rules.

nodes.title: Fail fast for invalid dates
nodes.why: Invalid inputs should produce a clear error instead of broken output.
nodes.alternative: Return an empty string on invalid input.
nodes.tradeoff: Throwing is strict and explicit, but callers must handle exceptions.

nodes.title: Use Intl with explicit time zone
nodes.why: Intl handles DST and zone rules correctly, which manual math usually gets wrong.
nodes.tradeoff: Intl is reliable and readable, but depends on runtime Intl support.

nodes.title: Extract stable parts instead of parsing a formatted string
nodes.why: `formatToParts` gives structured pieces, so output is not locale-string fragile.
nodes.alternative: Use `format()` and split the resulting string.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more code, but far more robust across locales.

nodes.title: Ignore literal separators
nodes.why: Only year/month/day values matter for the final YYYY-MM-DD output.
nodes.tradeoff: Filtering keeps map clean, but assumes required part keys exist.

nodes.title: Build deterministic YYYY-MM-DD output
nodes.why: Explicit assembly gives a consistent date string independent of locale order.
nodes.tradeoff: Custom assembly is predictable, but tied to available part names.
